title: Cerasus × syodoi
intro: Cerasus × syodoi, also known as Syodo's cherry, is a hybrid plant species within the genus Cerasus, belonging to the rose family Rosaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to improved cardiovascular health and immune function. Bioactive constituents of Cerasus × syodoi include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and anthocyanins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Cerasus × syodoi is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being used in the production of jams and preserves, taking advantage of its fruit's sweet and slightly tart flavor.
taxonomy: Cerasus × syodoi is a hybrid cherry tree. It belongs to the genus Cerasus, which is a part of the Rosaceae family. More specifically, Cerasus × syodoi is classified as a member of the subgenus Cerasus, within the genus. The exact classification of Cerasus × syodoi may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted to be a hybrid of two Cerasus species. This classification reflects the tree's unique characteristics and its position within the broader plant kingdom.
